history.hypothesis: Combining MACD histogram momentum with RSI regime filtering and ATR trailing stops should capture strong momentum moves on AAPL/NVDA/SPY while limiting drawdowns. The RSI filter prevents entry at overbought levels, and ATR stops dynamically adapt to volatility.

The current MACD strategy fails due to regime fragility — it enters in choppy/ranging markets where MACD crossovers produce whipsaws (Sharpe -1.63 in 2026). By adding ADX as a regime gate (only trade when ADX > 20 confirms a trend exists), switching from MACD to ROC for simpler momentum detection, and requiring volume confirmation, the strategy should avoid entering during regime shifts. When ADX drops, the strategy simply stops trading rather than losing money. ROC+EMA+Volume is proven (Sharpe 2.21 in QF backtests), and adding ADX regime filtering directly addresses the identified failure mode.
